title: Alsophila leptochlamys
taxonomy: Alsophila leptochlamys is classified within the family Cyatheaceae, commonly known as the tree fern family. This family is part of the larger division of Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that produce spores. Alsophila leptochlamys is a species of tree fern native to New Caledonia, an island country in the Pacific Ocean. The genus Alsophila is a diverse group of tree ferns, with over 60 species found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and reduce fever.
